turns.name: JOHN G. ROBERTS, JR.
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the Court today in case 22-535, Department of Education versus Brown.

turns.name: SAMUEL A. ALITO, JR.
turns.text: In August 2020, the Secretary of Education announced his intention to undertake a large-scale discharge of student loans. The Secretary cited his emergency powers under the Higher Education Relief Opportunities for Students Act of 2003. Among other things, that act authorizes the Secretary in some circumstances to dispense with certain procedures that he otherwise must follow before promulgating a rule, and the Secretary did not follow these procedures before promulgating the loan forgiveness. In this case, two borrowers who are dissatisfied with the scope of the loan forgiveness plan, claim that the act’s procedural exemptions do not apply to the loan forgiveness and that the Secretary was therefore required to follow customary procedures. That he did not, they argue, makes the loan forgiveness plan unlawful. The district court agreed that the loan forgiveness is unlawful and set it aside. But for the reasons discussed in our opinion today, we conclude that these borrowers lack standing to bring their claim. We therefore vacate the district court’s judgment and remand the case with instructions that it be dismissed. The opinion of the Court is unanimous.
